text.title: Embedding Cost Optimization Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every cost-opt config must enforce.

rule.statement: Dedup at ingest using SHA-256(text). Near-dup (cosine >0.98 on a cheap embedder) optionally collapsed.
rule.rationale: Re-ingested duplicates are the cheapest cost saved.

rule.statement: Embedding API calls batched: OpenAI ≤2048, Cohere ≤96, Voyage ≤128. Single-text calls rejected at lever-application time.
rule.rationale: Per-call overhead dominates at small batch.

rule.statement: Cache enabled when recurrence rate >5% per [[embedding-caching]] gate. Cache backend pinned in config.
rule.rationale: No cache on a recurrent corpus is leaving 30–60% savings on the table.

rule.statement: Dim reduction (e.g., text-embedding-3-large at dim 512) only if Recall@10 within 1pp of full-dim baseline.
rule.rationale: Reduction beyond the tested safe band loses recall.

rule.statement: For corpus >1M vectors: two-stage retrieval (cheap candidate set + expensive rerank) enabled when generator cost dominates.
rule.rationale: Single-stage full-corpus search costs more compute than candidate-set + rerank.

rule.statement: Monthly cost ≥$500 AND eval bench available → run the 5-step procedure.
Cost <$100/month OR no eval bench OR all levers already pulled → skip.
